3 Ideas You Might Have Missed
read time 2 minutes
Welcome to the Curiosity Chronicle Week in Review—a quick roundup of this week's ideas to help you build a high-performing, healthy, and wealthy life.
My book has been on the monthly NYT bestseller list for two consecutive months since release and has sold over 200,000+ copies worldwide. Get your copy now and join this movement to redefine success and wealth!
This Week at a Glance:
- Monday: The Mountain or The Pebble
- Wednesday: My Honest Advice on How to Stand Out
- Friday: The Paradox of Routines
Wisdom Worth Sharing:
"We must all suffer from one of two pains: the pain of discipline or the pain of regret. The difference is discipline weighs ounces while regret weighs tons." - Jim Rohn
Discipline is hard. Regret is hard.
But fortunately, you get to choose your hard.
([share to x/twitter!](https://click.convertkit-mail4.com/8kugoeez04coh2p92koi0fkdpp599a3/p8heh9h426l2qriq/aHR0cHM6Ly90d2l0dGVyLmNvbS9pbnRlbnQvdHdlZXQ_))
Monday: The Mountain or The Pebble
Are you blocked by the mountain in your path—or the pebble in your shoe?
Big obstacles are easy to blame, but it's the small, subtle frictions—negative mindsets, habits, relationships—that silently derail your progress. You can’t conquer your mountain until you remove your pebble.
([read the full piece here](https://click.convertkit-mail4.com/8kugoeez04coh2p92koi0fkdpp599a3/x0hph6hep2lpqoa5/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2FoaWxibG9vbS5jb20vbmV3c2xldHRlci90aGUtbW91bnRhaW4tb3ItdGhlLXBlYmJsZQ==))
Wednesday: 9 Uncommon Habits to Stand Out
The world rewards sameness, but you can choose to take the road less traveled.
From being anti-insecure to embracing your beginner’s mind, this brutally honest list of 9 traits will help you stand out in all the right ways.
([read the full piece here](https://click.convertkit-mail4.com/8kugoeez04coh2p92koi0fkdpp599a3/6qheh8hl5rd59rho/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2FoaWxibG9vbS5jb20vbmV3c2xldHRlci9teS1ob25lc3QtYWR2aWNlLW9uLWhvdy10by1zdGFuZC1vdXQ=))
Friday: The Paradox of Routines
When your routine becomes too rigid and complicated, it becomes a source of stress rather than strength.
The lesson is that your routines should have an A, B, and C level:
- A Level: Your ideal routine, if everything goes according to plan
- B Level: Your baseline routine, doable on an average day
- C Level: Your bare-bones routine, when all hell breaks loose
The best routine is the one that actually helps you get the important things done. Your routine is meant to serve you, not own you.
([read the full piece here](https://click.convertkit-mail4.com/8kugoeez04coh2p92koi0fkdpp599a3/kkhmh6hn5w35xvil/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2FoaWxibG9vbS5jb20vbmV3c2xldHRlci90aGUtcGFyYWRveC1vZi1yb3V0aW5lcw==))
